A test script for verifying try-catch behavior in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that checks the execution of a function named [`test`](<#14-002test>). The [`test`](<#14-002test>) function attempts to assign the value `1` to a variable `x` within a `try` block. If an error occurs during this operation, the `catch` block will return `false`. If no error occurs, the function returns `true`. The `assert` statement at the end of the script verifies that the [`test`](<#14-002test>) function returns `true`, indicating that the assignment operation was successful and no exceptions were thrown.

### test<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/12/12.14/12.14-002.test}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/12/12.14/12.14-002.js#L15>)

Checks if a variable assignment inside a try block executes without throwing an error.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Control Flow**:
    - Enter a try block.
    - Assign the value 1 to the variable `x`.
    - If an error occurs during the assignment, catch the error and return `false`.
    - If no error occurs, exit the try block and return `true`.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the assignment is successful, otherwise returns `false` if an error is caught.
